title: Rules of Work
description: How I regulate the process of making. These rules establish constraints that generate agency — each restriction creates space for discovery.

rules.rule: Constraints Create Constraints CreateConstraints CreateConstraints CreateConstraints Create
rules.author: Christian Schaible
rules.description: Every project begins with a rule that limits what you are allowed to do. The restriction is the starting point, not the obstacle.
rules.process: I gave myself exactly one typeface, one colour, and one hour per piece. The scarcity forced decisions that I would otherwise defer indefinitely.

rules.author: Christian Schaible
rules.description: No work may be evaluated until it is fully complete. Critique during production collapses the space between making and meaning.
rules.process: I covered the screen after each session and only reviewed the accumulated output at the end of the week. The distance changed what I saw.

rules.author: Christian Schaible
rules.description: A process is repeated verbatim until it produces a result that contradicts its own premise. That contradiction is the work.
rules.process: I printed the same layout forty times, changing nothing. By iteration thirty-two the ink registration had drifted enough to make every copy unique.
